title: NOC 72106 – Welders and related machine operators | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72106 professionals are the essential "fusing" force of Canadian industry. As a TEER 2 occupation, Welders use specialized equipment to join ferrous and non-ferrous metals, while related machine operators manage automated production lines for brazing and soldering. In 2026, as Canada scales up its National Shipbuilding Strategy, Electric Vehicle (EV) infrastructure, and Modular Housing initiatives, skilled welders—particularly those with robotic and high-pressure certifications—are classified as high-priority technical talent. For immigration, this role remains a cornerstone of the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and Category-Based Express Entry draws.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group721 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesMinor Group7210 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesNOC Code72106

duties: Interpretation: Read and interpret blueprints, welding process specifications, and engineering drawings.
Manual/Semi-Automatic Welding: Operate welding equipment to fuse metal segments using processes such as:GTAW (TIG welding)GMAW (MIG welding)FCAW (Flux-cored arc welding)SMAW (Stick welding)
Machine Operation: (For machine operators) Start up, shut down, and monitor previously set up production welding or brazing machines; adjust robotic welding heads according to specs.
Flame-Cutting: Operate manual or semi-automatic flame-cutting equipment to prepare metal parts.
Maintenance: Assist with the repair and maintenance of welding, brazing, and soldering equipment.
Quality Control: Inspect welded joints and structures for defects; repair worn parts of metal products by welding on extra layers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.

Welders:Completion of a 3-year apprenticeship program OR over 3 years of work experience in the trade plus college/industry courses.Trade certification is mandatory in Alberta and available (but voluntary) in all other provinces.
Completion of a 3-year apprenticeship program OR over 3 years of work experience in the trade plus college/industry courses.
Trade certification is mandatory in Alberta and available (but voluntary) in all other provinces.
Machine Operators:Some secondary school education is required.Several months of on-the-job training are provided.
Some secondary school education is required.
Several months of on-the-job training are provided.

Certifications (Mandatory/Highly Preferred):CWB (Canadian Welding Bureau) certification is required for structural welding.Red Seal Endorsement is the gold standard for PR and interprovincial work.H2S Alive or Standard First Aid for field-based oil/gas roles.
CWB (Canadian Welding Bureau) certification is required for structural welding.
Red Seal Endorsement is the gold standard for PR and interprovincial work.
H2S Alive or Standard First Aid for field-based oil/gas roles.
salary_prospects: Note: Specialized Pressure Welders or Pipeline Welders in Northern Canada or offshore projects often earn annual incomes exceeding $120,000 due to overtime and remote site premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. In British Columbia alone, over 3,100 job openings are forecasted through 2035, with the majority (85%) coming from the replacement of retiring workers. As 20% of the current workforce reaches retirement age by 2030, the demand for journey-level talent to mentor the next generation of apprentices is a top priority for the Canadian government.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Welders in 2026 is Stable to High, varying significantly by specialization. While general production welding in Ontario faces "limited" growth due to automation, there is a critical shortage of specialized welders in British Columbia (Shipbuilding), Alberta (Oil & Gas/Lithium), and Saskatchewan (Mining). According to 2026 labor market outlooks, employers are experiencing a 68% "hiring difficulty" rate for journey-level welders, making this a highly favorable time for international applicants with proven industrial experience.
Industrial Diversity: Work across high-growth sectors including aerospace precision welding, pipeline construction, and clean-tech manufacturing.
Robotic Integration: Lead the shift toward "Smart Factories" by managing and programming robotic welding cells and AI-monitored production lines.
Top-Tier Mobility: With a Red Seal Endorsement, you can work in any province or territory in Canada without needing further testing.
Direct PR Pathway: This trade is a permanent fixture in Express Entry Category-Based Selection for "Trade Occupations," often leading to faster invitations.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): The primary route for welders with at least 2 years of work experience.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Frequent draws for Welders supporting Ontario’s construction and manufacturing sectors.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A one-time measure for 33,000 temporary residents already in Canada, specifically targeting skilled trades like welding.
